anomaly.title: Apex Supplies (V-031) shares a registered address with employee Vikram Kulkarni (E-007)
anomaly.detail: Apex Supplies and Vikram Kulkarni share the exact registered address. 14 invoices totalling €332,000, all approved by E-007 (Vikram Kulkarni), 0% PO coverage, no tax ID.

payload.label: Apex Supplies (V-031) shares a registered address with employee Vikram Kulkarni (E-007)

anomaly.title: Deepak Verma (E-015) is paid to the same bank account as Vikram Kulkarni (E-007)
anomaly.detail: Two employees draw salary to one identical bank account. Deepak Verma has the thinner footprint (recent join / no other records) — a likely ghost employee whose pay is diverted to Vikram Kulkarni. €216,000 in payroll.

payload.label: Deepak Verma (E-015) is paid to the same bank account as Vikram Kulkarni (E-007)

anomaly.title: €87,400 paid 2× — INV-2025-0554 NEFT Vega Print Works - IN
anomaly.detail: The same €87,400 payment appears 2 times with no reversing credit note anywhere in the books — both debits reference the same invoice. Debits: "INV-2025-0554 NEFT Vega Print Works - INV-2025-0554" (BANK-STMT-2025-11) · "INV-2025-0554 NEFT Vega Print Works - INV-2025-0554" (BANK-STMT-2025-11). A likely duplicate-payment loss.

anomaly.title: €54,200 paid 2× — INV-2025-0229 NEFT Aravalli Components -
anomaly.detail: A duplicate payment of €54,200 — but a matching credit note reverses it. Likely a caught accounting error, not a loss. Verify the reversal. Debits: "INV-2025-0229 NEFT Aravalli Components - INV-2025-0229" (BANK-STMT-2025-07) · "INV-2025-0229 NEFT Aravalli Components - INV-2025-0229" (BANK-STMT-2025-07).

payload.query: €87,400 paid 2× — INV-2025-0554 NEFT Vega Print Works - IN
Apex Supplies (V-031) shares a registered address with employee Vikram Kulkarni
Deepak Verma (E-015) is paid to the same bank account as Vikram Kulkarni (E-007)

payload.text: Hypothesis: The duplicate payment of €54,200 to Aravalli Components was a legitimate accounting error that was successfully reversed via credit note CR-1042, resulting in no net financial loss. Before accusing, I need to rule out: The bank statement shows two identical debits, but credit note CR-1042 explicitly states the second payment was reversed during reconciliation, indicating a control catch rather than fraud.

Hypothesis: The second payment of €87,400 on 2025-11-13 for invoice INV-2025-0554 is a fraudulent duplicate payment because no credit note or reversal offsets it, resulting in a net cash loss. Before accusing, I need to rule out: The two payments represent a legitimate split payment arrangement (e.g., 50/50 deposit and balance) authorized by the vendor and approved by management, or the second payment is a corrected re-issue after the first was rejected by the bank (though the bank statement shows both cleared).

Hypothesis: Deepak Verma (E-015) is a fictitious employee created by Vikram Kulkarni (E-007) to divert €72,000/month into Vikram's personal bank account. Before accusing, I need to rule out: Deepak Verma is a legitimate new hire whose payroll setup was incorrectly linked to his manager's account due to a data entry error, or they are relatives sharing a joint account for legitimate personal reasons.

payload.query: Retrieve ledger entries for INV-2025-0229 and CR-1042 to confirm the credit note was poste

payload.text: Hypothesis: V-031 is a shell company controlled by E-007, evidenced by the shared address, self-approval of invoices, and lack of purchase orders or tax IDs. Before accusing, I need to rule out: The shared address is a legitimate shared business center or coworking space, and E-007 approved the invoices as part of standard procurement duties for a genuine external consultant, with POs filed under a different system or naming convention.

payload.query: Retrieve onboarding documents, signed employment contract, and IT email provisioning logs
Retrieve Purchase Order PO-69000 and payment approval logs for INV-2025-0554 to check for
Retrieve lease agreement or tenant list for 245 Avenue des Champs-Élysées and any alternat

payload.text: The existence of credit note CR-1042, which explicitly reverses the duplicate payment, combined with the absence of any further disbursements to the vendor in subsequent months, confirms that the duplicate was a caught accounting error with no net financial loss.

payload.summary: ✓ UPHELD by the Nemotron panel — 3/3 examiners upheld — All cited evidence directly and internally consistently confirms the ghost employee scheme: identical bank account with different home addresses rules out shared household, absence of employment recor

finding.statement: Deepak Verma (E-015) is a confirmed ghost employee. The HR records show E-015 and his manager Vikram Kulkarni (E-007) share the exact same bank account (BNP Paribas ****7731) despite having different home addresses, which rules out a shared household explanation. E-015 lacks a company email address, unlike all other peers, and the second-pass retrieval confirmed the total absence of an employment contract, onboarding documentation, or IT provisioning logs for E-015. This absence of foundational employment records, combined with the diversion of funds to the manager's account, confirms a payroll fraud scheme where Vikram Kulkarni created a fictitious employee to siphon €72,000 per month.

evidence.claim: E-015 and E-007 share the same bank account (BNP Paribas ****7731).
E-015 and E-007 have different home addresses, ruling out a shared household/joint account explanation.
E-015 has no company email on file, while peers (E-007, E-016) do.
No employment contract, onboarding docs, or IT logs exist for E-015 in the company books.
E-007 is the reporting manager for E-015, providing opportunity to create and approve the ghost payroll.
E-015 is listed as Active with a monthly salary of €72,000.

nemotron.reasoning: 3/3 examiners upheld — All cited evidence directly and internally consistently confirms the ghost employee scheme: identical bank account with different home addresses rules out shared household, absence of employment recor
